evidence.scientific_clarity: 6-cluster ridgeline KDE plot clearly shows progressive mode shift from ~1.9 (Cluster 1) to ~3.4 (Cluster 6), directly carrying the brief's scientific claim with good readability.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none' set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVuSans — the matplotlib default explicitly flagged as unacceptable; text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.palette: Sequential 6-color rainbow (purple→blue→teal→green→yellow→orange) is largely colorblind-safe with no red/green directional misuse, but yellow (#d4c03a) adjacent to green may be hard to distinguish for deuteranopes.
evidence.layout_density: Overlap factor 0.55 stacks ridges efficiently without crowding; left-axis cluster labels are well-placed; y-range is tight; no gaping whitespace or label collisions visible.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes the figure; cluster labels serve as legend; Reviewer 2 might request explicit axis tick labels on y or a colorbar, and the missing fonttype=42 would be flagged in a print-ready submission.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains only a Type 3 (bitmap-embedded) DejaVuSans font; pdf.fonttype=42 is absent from the script, so text is not truly vector/selectable; fails the vector fidelity requirement.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (50 KB), figure.png (74 KB), caption.tex (479 B), gen_figure.py (2 KB); bonus figure.svg also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption specifies plot type, n per cluster (~400–500), KDE bandwidth (Scott's rule), peak normalization, cluster ordering, and gives a precise numerical finding (mode shift 1.9→3.4) — fully informative.

overall_strengths: Excellent caption with specific numerical findings, methodology details, and clear description matching the figure.
Clean ridgeline layout with well-chosen overlap factor, clear left-axis labels, and all required output files present.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype='none'); PDF text is rendered as Type 3 bitmap fonts (DejaVu Sans default), making it non-selectable and not truly vector.
Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout with no explicit font configuration — violates the typography requirement and may look unprofessional in publication.
